what_it_does: Declares a human-readable metadata registry (NODE_TYPE_META) that maps internal node-type identifiers to display names and short 'is' descriptions, intended to be read first by an instrument so users see meaning rather than machine ids.
observations: Contains a single dictionary NODE_TYPE_META keyed by node-type strings such as ask, codebase, constant, embed, gate, join, llm, etc.
Each entry follows the same shape {name, is}: a human-facing name and a one-sentence description of what the node type does.
Heavily commented: describes itself as a 'NODE-TYPE LEGIBILITY REGISTRY' and a mirror of kinds/raw.py using composition's 'ONE legibility-type, MANY registries' pattern.
Includes a fallback rule for unseeded node types (humanize the id) but does not implement it here.
Marked as a TENTATIVE draft awaiting Tim/DNA ratification.
Explicitly states meaning lives in the data/registry, not in the instrument.
Notes that composition's validate/backfill set-diff can detect node types present in the registry but missing here.
Discusses a future migration from central map to per-module META declarations on each node module.
standouts: The file is purely declarative/human-interpretive: no functions, classes, or logic beyond the data map.
It encodes a policy that the operator should never see the machine node-id, only name + is.
It positions itself as a reversible seed toward a declared-at-birth per-module metadata pattern.
concerns: The registry is labeled TENTATIVE and draft, so it may be out of sync with actual node modules.
It references a validation/backfill process and per-module META migration that are not implemented or evidenced in this file.
No fallback implementation or validation code is present, only a stated intent.
notes: This file is the human meaning layer for node types: a single data map that gives every node type a legible name and description, meant to be consumed declaratively by rendering tools.
questions: Which node types are currently present in the system but missing from this registry?
Where and how is the fallback humanization of unseeded node types implemented?
How will composition's validate/backfill set-diff be run, and what triggers it?
Will the per-module META migration happen, and what will read those per-module declarations?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches what it does: it declares a human-readable node-type registry. The file also contains design notes and future migration plans that go beyond the current data map, but those are commentary rather than behavior.
apparent_themes: Composition pattern: one shared shape, many registries
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish a single source of human meaning for node types so that UI/instruments can render them without hardcoding labels, while leaving the door open to move the metadata closer to each node module's definition later.
novelty: It treats human-readable node-type labels as a first-class registry with a uniform {name, is} shape and explicitly forbids the instrument from owning the meaning.
contribution: Provides a centralized, easy-to-read lookup table of node-type semantics that any fused system can consume to render node types to humans; contributes the interpretive layer and the policy that meaning belongs in data, not in the rendering code.
summary_for_embedding: company/nodes/_meta.py defines NODE_TYPE_META, a centralized registry mapping node-type identifiers to human-readable names and one-sentence descriptions for use by rendering instruments.
intention_for_embedding: This file was meant to be the declared-first interpretive layer that lets users see legible node-type names and meanings rather than internal machine ids.
suggested_fields: node_type_source: whether each entry was derived from its node module docstring or invented
registry_coverage: list of node types known elsewhere but absent here
fallback_humanizer: the actual implementation location for unseeded ids
proposed_edge_kinds: mirrors: registry mirrors the structure of another registry (e.g., kinds/raw.py)
declares_meaning_for: registry entry declares human meaning for a node type
seeds_future: file is a temporary seed intended to be replaced by per-module META
